What does mean?
"The radar is scanning high up in the clouds this far out so these winds are likely not the same at the surface."
it means, just becaude they're reading 125mph winds (for example), we likely wouldn't see that same speed on the ground
Radar cannot scan in a flat line, it increases in height the further out it is scanning. So it is not possible for it to scan what is going on close to ground until it gets close.
Radar is scanning from above, It is difficult to gauge wind speed at surface/ground level from the radar which is above hurricane.
my understanding that the winds in the high clouds will not be as strong on the surface.
right....is the difference better or worse....
the highest winds are higher up in a storm, not on the surface level of the storm. These numbers are from high up, not surface level.
Ikr. Doesn’t say if they’re stronger or not.
